results.title: Jeffrey
results.creator_credit: Christopher Ashley, Mark Balsam, Mitchell Maxwell, Victoria Maxwell, The Booking Office and Working Man Films
results.brief_description: This film manages to convey a serious message about the impact of “safe sex” on gay male relationships despite its surreal and comedic approach. Fed up with paranoid lovers, endless bundles of blood test results, and the consuming fear of investing emotionally in a partner he may lose to AIDS, Jeffrey decides to give up sex for good. He rationalises that this will allow him to channel his energy into his career and hobbies, which in turn will help him to forget about sex. However, his resolve is soon put to the test when he meets the gorgeous body-builder Steve (Michael T. Weiss) while working out at the gym. Torn between wanting to ravish Steve and wanting to maintain his celibacy, Jeffrey turns to his best friend Sterling (Patrick Stewart) for advice. As circumstances (and friends) conspire to bring Steve and Jeffrey together, Jeffrey begins to warm to the idea of a committed relationship. However, Steve’s revelation about his HIV-positive status soon throws a spanner in the works and Jeffrey finds himself caught between love and fear once again. Seeking the advice of Sterling and his partner Darius, a post-modern evangelist, a self-help group, his liberal parents, and a randy Catholic priest, Jeffrey struggles to come to terms with the concept that a life lived in fear is no life at all. Features a variety of cameos from stars such as Sigourney Weaver, Camryn Manheim, Olympia Dukakis, Kathy Najimy, Christine Baranski, and Nathan Lane. Based on the play by Paul Rudnick.

results.title: The Tao of Steve
results.creator_credit: Jenniphr Goodman, Anthony Bregman and Thunderhead Productions
results.brief_description: Santa Fe primary school teacher Dex (Donal Logue) is a success with the ladies and he owes it all to Steve - Steve Austin, Steve McQueen, Steve McGarrett - the prototypical cool American male, he never has to impress the women but always gets the ladies. Dex and his entourage of single male friends (one is happily married), subscribe to the tao of Steve, valuing such ideals as suppressing that which you desire and doing something excellent in front of the ladies. Dex’s comfortable, philandering existence is thrown into turmoil when he meets Syd (Greer Goodman) - a conquest from his thinner, college years - whom, to his surprise, he discovers he is falling in love with, causing him to question the code by which he has lived his life thus far. A surprise sleeper at the 2000 Sundance Film Festival, Goodman’s directorial debut saw Donal Logue awarded with a Special Jury Prize.

results.title: Christ stopped at Eboli = Cristo si e' fermato a Eboli
results.creator_credit: Franco Cristaldi, Nicola Carraro, Francesco Rosi, Radiotelevisione Italiana, TV2 and VIDES Cinematografica
results.brief_description: This four part mini-series (which was also released as a feature film in a condensed form) is based on the autobiography of Carlo Levi, writer, painter, doctor and avowed anti-fascist. In 1935 Levi was exiled to Lucania in the extreme south of Italy for his political activities. In this remote peasant land so cut off from the domain of history that even Christ never arrived, so the locals say, Levi discovered a way of life which transformed him. He felt more strongly the weight of age-old injustices and went back to the roots of these injustices. ‘This film is a journey through my own conscience and through that of all men who carry a Lucania in themselves’ (Rosi). Cast includes: Gian Maria Volonte, Paolo Bonacelli, Alain Cuny, Irene Papas. Written by Francesco Rosi, Tonino Guerra, Raffaele la Capria ; photography, Pasqualino De Santis ; music, Piero Piccioni. Italian dialogue with English subtitles.

results.title: Fear
results.creator_credit: James Foley, Brian Grazer, Ric Kidney and Image Entertainment
results.brief_description: An intense psychological thriller about violence amongst teens starring Mark Wahlberg, Reese Witherspoon, Alyssa Milano, William Petersen and Amy Brenneman. Nicole Walker (Witherspoon) is a typical high school student who lives with her affluent and overprotective father (Petersen) and his new wife (Brenneman). Rebelling against him, Nicole sneaks out of the house with her best friend Margo (Milano) to a club, where she meets attractive bad-boy David McCall (Wahlberg in one of his first lead roles). Despite her father’s reservations about her first serious boyfriend and his questionable friends, Nicole falls head-over-heels for him and is shocked when his true possessive and violent nature is revealed. Ending their relationship, she tries to move on with her life. David has other plans, stalking Nicole and terrorising her family and friends, culminating in an attack on Nicole’s home. Set in Seattle, ‘Fear’ shows just how easy a seemingly perfect lifestyle can be altered by violence, with Nicole’s wealthy, privileged lifestyle overlapping with David’s underworld grunge life.

results.title: Smokey and the bandit
results.creator_credit: Hal Needham, Mort Engelberg, Rastar Films and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: When maverick truck driver “Bandit” (Burt Reynolds) is approached by a father and son team at a Truck Rodeo, it sparks the beginning of a wild ride from Georgia to Texas. The challenge is for Bandit to drive from one state to the other, collect a truckload of Coors beer, and return with the precious load within 28 hours. The challenge has been laid before but never completed. The reward is $80,000, the danger lies in the fact that his actions qualify as bootlegging (taking beer across state lines without a permit is illegal). Bandit enlists the help of his buddy, “The Snowman” (Jerry Reed), while Bandit rides shotgun in a 1976 Black Trans Am - Bandit One. Before long a damsel in distress joins the small crew when Bandit picks up hitchhiking runaway bride Carrie (Sally Field). Carrie was supposed to be marrying Junior, and her future father-in-law, Sheriff Buford T Justice (Jackie Gleason), aka “The Bandit”, is soon in pursuit of his son’s intended. Whilst it didn’t receive much critical success, this slice of 70s nostalgia is laden with silly and sexist one liners, impressive car chase sequences, a fondness for the culture of CB radios and an anti-authoritarian stance. Having said that, it also took $126+ million at the box office.

results.title: Mallrats
results.creator_credit: Kevin Smith, James Jacks, Laura Greenlee, Scott Mosier, Sean Daniel, Alphaville, Gramercy Pictures and View Askew Productions
results.brief_description: When T.S. (Jeremy London) arrives to pick up his girlfriend Brandi (Claire Forlani) for their planned vacation, she cancels their plans to be a contestant on her father’s game show. Hurt by Brandi’s rejection, T.S. visits her neighbour and his friend Brodie (Jason Lee), who also has just been dumped by his girlfriend Rene (Shannon Doherty). The two head to the mall in an attempt to console themselves. When they discover that Brandi’s father’s show is being recorded in the mall that very day, they enlist the help of troublemakers Jay (Jason Mewes) and Silent Bob (director Kevin Smith) to stop the show. The sequel to Kevin Smith’s hit film “Clerks”, and the second film in his ‘New Jersey’ series. Cast also includes Ben Affleck, Joey Lauren Adams, Renee Humphrey, Ethan Suplee and comic book tycoon Stan Lee.

results.title: Cleopatra [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Joseph L Mankiewicz, Walter Wanger and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Elizabeth Taylor, Richard Burton and Rex Harrison star in this tale of power and betrayal - the legendary story of the Queen of the Nile and her conquest of Julius Caesar and Marc Antony. An unforgettable portrayal of the beguiling beauty who seduced two of Rome’s greatest soldiers and changed the course of history. Also stars: Pamela Brown, George Cole, Hume Cronyn, Cesare Danova, Kenneth Haigh, Andrew Keir, Martin Landau, Roddy McDowall, Robert Stephens and Francesca Annis. Music by Alex North. Screenplay by Joseph L. Mankiewicz, Ranald MacDougall and Sidney Buchman. Cinematography by Leon Shamroy. (Academy Awards: Cinematography, Art direction, Costumes, Sets and Special effects)

results.title: Paris when it sizzles
results.creator_credit: Richard Quine, George Axelrod and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Richard Benson is an aging screenwriter and a cynical lush who has been commissioned to write a screenplay for Alexander Meyerheimer, a powerful Hollywood producer. Benson has spent several paid weeks in France drinking and carousing, and so far has only managed to conjure up the opening credits. Benson engages the services of typist Gabrielle Simpson to assist with the task of writing the script, which is due in two days. As Gabrielle and Richard brainstorm ideas for the film the various scenarios and plot contrivances that they conjure up are played before the audience, making “Paris when it sizzles”, the classic film within a film. The two collaborators run the gamut of movie character cliche - playboy, gangster, ingenue, vampire, femme fatale, the hooker with the heart of gold - imagining themselves in the key roles. Set in Paris, the film sends up techniques such as the dissolve, the cross and double cross, and the final kiss that seduces filmgoers as well as having digs at the French New wave and method acting. Despite being a romantic comedy, “Paris when it sizzles” takes a rather cynical view of the filmmaking business. Features cameos from Marlene Dietrich and Tony Curtis.

results.title: An Affair to remember [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Leo McCarey, Jerry Wald and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Debonair playboy Nickie Ferrante (Cary Grant) and beautiful nightclub singer Terry McKay (Deborah Kerr) meet travelling first class aboard a transatlantic luxury liner courtesy of two other people. She is a tycoon’s mistress and he is engaged to an heiress. Their sudden, but undeniable love affair leads them to make a vow - that they will break off their current relationships, get jobs and meet in six months to continue their affair. However, a tragic accident prevents Terry from keeping their rendezvous and the lovers future takes an emotional and uncertain turn. This film is a remake of Leo McCarey’s “Love Story” and Vic Damone croons the title tune on the soundtrack.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Leo McCarey, Jerry Wald and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.description: Debonair playboy Nickie Ferrante (Cary Grant) and beautiful nightclub singer Terry McKay (Deborah Kerr) meet travelling first class aboard a transatlantic luxury liner courtesy of two other people. She is a tycoon’s mistress and he is engaged to an heiress. Their sudden, but undeniable love affair leads them to make a vow - that they will break off their current relationships, get jobs and meet in six months to continue their affair. However, a tragic accident prevents Terry from keeping their rendezvous and the lovers future takes an emotional and uncertain turn. This film is a remake of Leo McCarey’s “Love Story” and Vic Damone croons the title tune on the soundtrack.

results.title: Gentleman's agreement [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Elia Kazan, Darryl F. Zanuck and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Phil Green (Gregory Peck) is a single father and journalist lured to New York as a feature writer for a magazine. Assigned to write about anti-semitism, he struggles to find the right angle from which to explore this issue from a more personal perspective. Unrelenting in his quest for truth, Phil poses as a Jew and encounters prejudice first hand. Determined to expose intolerance and hypocrisy, he confronts those who blatantly practice anti-semitism and challenges those close to him who support it by remaining silent. Includes original theatrical trailer and Movietone News footage. Cast also includes Dorothy McGuire, John Garfield, Celeste Holm, Anne Revere, June Havoc and Dean Stockwell. Screenplay by Moss Hart, based on the original story by Laura Z. Hobson. Cinematography by Arthur Miller. Music by Alfred Newman. (Academy Awards: Best Film, Best Director, Best Supporting Actress)
